name: Allen Toussaint
description: American musician, songwriter and record producer (1938–2015)
abstract: Allen Richard Toussaint (; January 14, 1938 – November 10, 2015) was an American musician, songwriter, arranger, and record producer. He was an influential figure in New Orleans rhythm and blues from the 1950s to the end of the century, described as "one of popular music's great backroom figures." Many musicians recorded Toussaint's comp…

about_quotes.speaker: James Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: when we worked with Allen Toussaint he was quite what do you call it, impressed that I’d even heard of them
about_quotes.doc_title: James Hunter interviewed by Monk Rowe, Boston, Massachusetts, July 25, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Elvis Costello, and the interviewer brings up Allen Toussaint in the context of Costello's work. The interviewee does not mention Toussaint directly or describe a personal relationship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 21, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Most of the rhythm & blues sessions were done, the biggest studio was probably Allen Toussaint’s studio at Sea-Saint. And Allen was the piano player. ... So I was never called to do any of that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: David Torkanowsky
about_quotes.evidence: I mean Allen Toussaint sounded very French, very ornate, very French.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Torkanowsky interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019
